sources_exist.note: All nine distinct URLs underlying the 13 canonical citation occurrences resolved to the cited pages with accessible content.

figures_match_sources.note: Several component facts are present: Envato reports 40% daily AI use among graphic designers in a global creative-professional survey; Oberlo reports 37% of small-business owners are age 55-73; Peak reports 3.21x-4.30x advertising-agency EBITDA; FE reports 8x-12x for agencies above $5M EBITDA; and the pricing guide describes hourly, project, and retainer models. The sources do not establish the selected NAICS-specific adoption, billing shares, transaction multiples, active-consolidator count, or the claimed 1985-1992 and 2003-2007 majority-adoption timelines.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The role fractions, pi_dist, and pi_moat are bounded and directionally reflect the prompt's product-replacement warning. However, t50_years is internally inconsistent: the derivation says 35% current adoption and 6-8 percentage points per year imply roughly 2-3 years to 50%, then selects 3.5 years without a stated adjustment. The reweighted six-role labor structure also uses exact 6-digit shares without an auditable derivation from the supplied 4-digit role mix.

rubric_consistent.note: The deterministic dataset inputs match exactly and the MELTING classification follows the frozen industry warning. The BORDERLINE flag is explicitly resolved: S=3.020311 is 0.0203 above the 3.00 cut, but C=0.60 is independently below the frozen 1.0 kill floor, so proximity to the pass cut cannot rescue the result. The ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag is non-disqualifying by itself, but unsupported inputs feeding A, B, C, and M prevent acceptance.

cross_checks_honest.note: All four required cross-checks are present and candid about product replacement, the no-proxy rule, and rapid project/hourly price deflation. The billing proportions remain unsupported, but the stated mechanism agrees with pi_dist=0.30, pi_moat=0.20, and MELTING.

source_audits.note: FE describes broad advertising/marketing agency consolidation and a generic PE platform-and-bolt-on playbook. It does not name a PE-backed graphic-design platform active in NAICS 541430 or support the selected count of one marginal consolidator.

Peak states an advertising-agency EBITDA range of 3.21x-4.30x. It gives no graphic-design-studio transactions, $1-10M EBITDA segmentation, median paid multiple, or owner-comp normalization, and therefore does not establish the selected 4.5x buy multiple.

Envato reports 40% daily AI use for graphic designers within a global survey of 1,780 creative professionals. It does not measure US firm-level material adoption in NAICS 541430 or the separate Gitnux 35% agency-integration figure, so it cannot establish the blended 35% input.

FE reports 8x-12x for agencies above $5M EBITDA and 10.1x broad North America/Europe median EV/EBITDA, while emphasizing premiums for performance, data, martech, and recurring revenue. It provides no $10M+ EBITDA graphic-design platform comp and does not establish the discounted 7.5x exit input.

Novedge supports PageMaker's 1985 introduction and notes that InDesign later became an industry standard. It contains no 1992 majority/dominance date, no measured adoption curve, and no 2003-2007 Adobe-CS-versus-Quark 50% handover.

Oberlo reports 37% of US small-business owners in the broad 55-73 bracket in 2024. The record clearly labels 28% age 60+ as an estimate from that cohort and acknowledges the absence of a design-specific breakdown; this supports the disclosed general-population fallback basis, though not a direct industry measurement.

The guide explains hourly, project, retainer, value, performance, and equity pricing and shows how hourly billing favors client price pressure. It provides no graphic-studio revenue mix and does not establish the asserted 75% project/hourly and 25% retainer split used in pi_dist.
Envato supports substantial AI use among designers, but it does not measure client ownership, switching costs, fee compression, billing mix, or the selected pi_dist=0.30.

Axial reports digital/marketing-agency valuation ranges and discusses client retention, concentration, and service-business characteristics. It does not quantify graphic-design billing mix or the extent to which AI savings flow to clients, so it does not establish pi_dist=0.30.

The global creative-professional survey shows graphic designers using AI daily, which supports competitive exposure, but it does not measure NAICS 541430 switching costs, client self-service, studio portability, or moat strength.

The accessible article expressly reports that Fiverr's active buyer count dropped 10% in 2024 while discussing platform AI changes. This supports the exact factual signal cited in the pi_moat rationale, though the article does not prove AI caused the decline.

Gitnux states 35% global design-agency AI integration by mid-2024, but provides only report-level attribution across 100 datasets, including dubious/non-resolving domain labels, with no claim-to-source mapping. The exact statistic cannot be verified in context and the page does not establish moat strength.

The page supports the existence and mechanics of hourly, project, and retainer billing for creative freelancers. It does not support the quoted logo, website, hourly, or retainer ranges and supplies no evidence for the selected 50/25/25 revenue split among small graphic-design studios.

reasons: At inputs.t50_years.derivation, 35% current adoption plus a stated 6-8 percentage-point annual adoption pace implies about 1.9-2.5 years to reach 50%, yet the record selects 3.5 years without a documented adjustment. Re-run with a coherent date baseline and explicit calculation from accessible adoption and analog evidence.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://novedge.com/blogs/design-news/design-software-history-aldus-pagemaker-revolutionizing-design-with-desktop-publishing-in-the-1980s), the page does not support 1992 majority adoption or a 2003-2007 Adobe/Quark 50% transition. Re-run with sources that measure the asserted adoption milestones rather than product-release history.

At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://elements.envato.com/learn/ai-trend-report), a global person-level daily-use statistic does not establish US firm-level material adoption in NAICS 541430, and the additional 35% agency figure is not supported by that URL. Re-run with a traceable firm-level source or a fully documented conversion and population adjustment.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url and inputs.pi_dist.sources[0] (https://www.creativeagencybook.com/blog/best-pricing-models-for-creative-services), the page describes pricing models but not the 50% project, 25% hourly, 25% retainer revenue mix that drives capture. Re-run with survey evidence for shares or an explicit defensible estimate chain.

At inputs.buy_mult.url (https://peakbusinessvaluation.com/advertising-agency-valuation-multiples/) and inputs.exit_mult.url (https://www.feinternational.com/blog/agency-marketing-ma-consolidation-ai-exit-opportunities), the evidence covers broad advertising/marketing agencies rather than paid graphic-design transactions at the required small-target and $10M+ platform scopes. Re-run with matched transaction comps or a documented comparable normalization, including size and business-model adjustments.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://www.feinternational.com/blog/agency-marketing-ma-consolidation-ai-exit-opportunities), broad agency PE activity does not identify any platform specifically doing graphic-design add-ons in the last 24 months and cannot support active_consolidators=1. Re-run with named platform/add-on evidence and qualifying dates.

At inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role, the exact reweighted labor shares (including 40% graphic designers, 6.5% art directors, and 25.5% admin/tail) are not derived from the supplied role mix or an auditable 6-digit source. Re-run with a transparent crosswalk/scaling calculation before applying within-role fractions.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S
